name: Noxious Weeds Program Infestations
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This dataset displays the known locations of noxious weed infestations across King County, Washington. It includes both active infestations, which are currently present and monitored, and non-active infestations, where weeds were previously reported but may have been controlled, eradicated, or require no further management at this time. name: King County NWI Wetlands with LLWW, HGM and Function Attributes
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.The project, entitled “King County Wetland Inventory Update, King County, WA ” used geospatial techniques and image interpretation processes to remotely map and classify wetlands (includes deepwater habitats) and riparian areas in King County, WA. name: Stream Type
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Stream Type is a watercourse layer that identifies the regulatory stream type per the County’s definitions in the Critical Areas Ordinance. The baseline data for the physical stream location comes from the Coho Intrinsic Potential stream layer, which was based on the county’s ‘watercourse’ layer circa 2020. name: Stormwater Facilities
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Inventory of all stormwater flow control, water quality, and regional stormwater facilities that are inspected or maintained by King County. The layer displays the boundary of the facility based on assets that are included within the facility.

name: Facility Features Access Road
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This feature class contains access road features assocatied with stormwater facilities. These assets require tracking but are not specifically part of the MS4 framework.

name: Facility Features Fencing
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This feature class contains fence and gate features assocatied with stormwater facilities. These assets require tracking but are not specifically part of the MS4 framework.

name: Facility Features Bollard
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This feature class contains bollard features assocatied with stormwater facilities. These assets require tracking but are not specifically part of the MS4 framework.

name: King County Metro Comfort Stations
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Contains names, locations, hours and contact information of restroom facilities accessible to transit operators.

name: Fish Passage Site Watersheds
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.King County fish passage barrier prioritization scoring and Level B assessment data processing requires site-specific drainage basins. This dataset includes the area of land where precipitation collects and drains to a particular site. name: Revegetation Tracker Projects in King County
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This dataset has summary data for revegetation projects in King County. The projects are tracked from planning to planting and maintenace phases. Different agencies in King County help track these projects. Each project has a summary of trees and shrubs planted and the most recent phase of each project.

name: Farmland Preservation Program Parcels
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Parcels within the Farmland Preservation Program for which the development rights have been sold and the property is restricted to agricultural use.

name: Farmland Preservation Program Farm Boundaries
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Contains farm boundaries containing one or more parcels within the Farmland Preservation Program with the same farm number for which the development rights have been sold and the property is restricted to agricultural use.

name: King County Metro Stops
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.On-street location where transit vehicles stop inline to pick-up and discharge passengers. It has a sign and basic service information; sometimes also a shelter with benches.

name: King County Metro Routes
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.King County Metro route footprint. A portion of the scheduled operation of a transit vehicle that is advertised in public timetables as available for public transportation service.

name: King County Metro Pattern
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Contains revenue patterns for the current service period. Transit clients can use this layer to display all the revenue & deadhead patterns in the current & next service periods. Used for analysis and display, as the patterns are linked to routes

Interior Dimensions 23" Wide x 42" Long x Variable Depth, 12’ Max.; Max Depth to Pipe invert is 9’ 6”. name: Small Habitat Restoration Project (SHRP) Data
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.King County's Small Habitat Restoration Projects are low cost projects in rural and urban King County that enhance and restore streams and wetlands. Typical projects include streamside and wetland planting, livestock fencing, in-stream habitat improvements, removal of barriers to fish migration and removal of invasive/non-native plants.

name: Water Quality Swale
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Component Types-Basic biofiltration swale: A long, gently sloped, vegetated ditch designed to filter pollutants from stormwater. name: Facility Features Polygon
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This layer maps features such as mowing areas and other features that mapped as polygons and are part of stormwater facilities.

name: Facility Features Point
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This layer is used to map point based features that are part of facilities that are not part of the stormwater framework but are included in the county inventory becasue they still require maintenance actions. An example would be a facility sign.

name: Facility Features Line
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This feature class contains facility features such as fence lines, check dams and other linear features that require tracking but are not specifically part of the MS4 framework.

name: Control Other Line
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Control Other Line Component Type---Infiltration Trench: 2-5’ wide trench designed to infiltrate stormwater below the ground surface. They feature perforated distribution pipes that is topped with filter fabric to prevent dispersal to the ground surface.---Bioretention Swale: May have rocks in the swale, various plantings with a landscaped feature look. ---Dispersal Trench: 2.5’ or greater trench designed to disperse stormwate

name: Control Filter Strip
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Filter strips provide a grassy area with gentle slopes which treats stormwater runoff from adjacent paved areas before it concentrates into discrete channels.

name: Conveyance Open
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Componets of Open ConveyanceTrench Drain: Collects and conveys runoff. Has a grated top and a Pipe or box bottom, typically linear. Curb/Gutter: Collects and conveys runoff. A Curb/Gutter is the raised edge or perimeter barrier of a roadway or other hard surface

name: Open Conveyance Ditch and Natural Drainage
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Components of Open Conveyance (Ditch and Natural Drainage)--Ditch: A long narrow constructed channel.--Natural Drainage: Non-engineered, non-constructed flow paths following existing land contours. When a stream isn’t mapped and the natural drainage system (stream) may be confused with a ditch that is part of King County’s MS4, the stream is mapped as Natural Drainage to prevent future confusion.

name: Closed Conveyance
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Closed Conveyance ComponentsPipe: A circular closed pipe that is designed to convey stormwater either by gravity or pressurizedflow that is connected to a connect feature on one or both ends of the pipe.Culvert: A Closed Conveyance that drains open channels, Swales, or Ditches for the purpose of passing under a roadway, embankment or other structure. Culverts are not connected to a structure on one or both ends.

name: Connect Unknown Intersect
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Components--Unknown: A connector that staff cannot define because the structure is buried, paved over, flooded, etc. The location is often inferred. Unknown is for use in the field.--Intersection: A connector point whose location is inferred as the convergence between one or more stormwater conveyance structures (e.g. a pipe) where there is believed to be no catch basin or other structure that serves as a connector.

name: Connect Undefined Type
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Undefined features are components that have not been created in the stormwater framework this could comprise of new stormwater designs or potentially archaic features that were not included in the development of the stormwater framework.

name: Connect Pump
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Pumps are connect function type which are powered or mechanical device that conveys stormwater from a lower elevation to a higher elevation along a specified length.

name: Connect Access
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Components of Access Features--Access Riser: A vertical maintenance access to a Vault, Tank or other similar structure. name: Concept Link
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Component Types of Links-- Connection: The location where a discharge enters or leaves the Permittee’s MS4 from or to a private entity, other public agency, or other jurisdiction. It also includes discharges from the Permittee’s MS4 that occur above ground and are not within the ordinary high water mark of a receiving water. name: King County Public Benefit Rating System Current Use Taxation Properties
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.The Public Benefit Rating System (PBRS) and the Timber Land programs provide incentives to encourage landowners to voluntarily conserve and protect land resources, open space and timber.

name: Water Sampling sites
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Location of sampling locations for water samples collected by the King County Environmental Laboratory

name: Parcel
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This layer represents the tax parcels in King County. This layer also includes place-holder polygons which do not represent tax parcels but are included to identify platted and unplatted land descriptions. The layer includes stacked polygon geometry for undivided interest and vertical parcels. King County parcel numbers may include leading zeros in PIN, Major or Minor. The data is created for Assessment purposes and is not gua

name: Rights of Way
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This layer shows the Rights of Way polygons as they are represented in the King County Parcel layer. The polygons in this layer are generated from the ROAD and RDNA coding in the parcel layer. This layer is for cartographic use and not intended to reflect the official designation of a road for transportation purposes. Polygons in this layer are not tax parcels.

name: Wastewater Conveyance of King County
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.All of the conveyance involved in treatment of wastewater for King County Wastewater Treatment Division

name: Wastewater Treatment Facilities of King County
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.All of the maintenance holes, pump stations, regulator stations, and treatment plants involved in conveyance and treatment of wastewater for King County WTD

name: Groundwater Sources
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Locations of groundwater sources organized into 3 categories (as defined by the attribute SRC_TYPE) providing all known sources whether domestic or public water systems (PWS). PWS are either Group A with 15 or more connections or Group B with 2 to 14 connections, as recorded by the WA Department of Health.

name: King County Hydrological Monitoring Gauges
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.This dataset shows the locations of King County maintained hydrological gauges from 1994 to the present (except for Water Temperature gauges which are listed from 1988 to present).

name: Tree Canopy 2021 - Tree Points
description: This layer provides an approximate inventory of tree center points for King County in 2021. Tree points were derived through an automated process using 2021 lidar, Ecopia land cover, and NAIP imagery. This layer provides an estimate of tree location and tree height from lidar, tree type (evergreen vs decidious) from lidar intensity, and vegetation health from imagery using NDVI.The points in this layer have not been QA/QC'd or field verified. description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Urban and Rural Designated areas of King County as established in the 1994 King County Comprehensive Plan. Boundaries have been conflated to parcel boundaries current in 2016. This data layer was created to support a change analysis for Urban Growth Area change from 1994 to 2016.

name: Orthophoto Ground Control Locations and Image Error Calculations
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Location of painted targets, as point features, used during aerial imagery photo acquisition. Data comes from multiple sources, and various vintages. Older vintage locations may not be visible in newer imagery, especially imagery greater than 1-foot resolution. Locations contain x,y and z coordinates.

name: Potential Annexation Areas (PAA) and Other Urban Unincorporated Areas of King County
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Shows unincorporated (outside city) areas with an urban designation under the King County comprehensive plan (KCCP). Including: Designated City PAAs (areas planned to be annexed to a city in the future); City in Rural Area UGAs (similar but associated with the six cities in the rural area designated in the KCCP); Urban Planned Development (UPD, Redmond Ridge); and other misc. categories.

name: Health Reporting Areas 2020
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Health Reporting Areas (HRAs) are aggregates of Census 2020 blocks created to facilitate the analysis and presentation of sub-county health statistics. Where possible, HRAs are defined as neighborhoods within large cities, smaller cities, unincorporated areas in King County, or a combination of these geographies.

name: High Capacity and Frequent Transit Stops
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.High capacity and frequent transit stops used to create a half-mile walkshed area.  Used to determine density and dimensional development standards in unincorporated King County as detailed in KCC chapter 21A.12.  These transit stops are mapped by the Metro Transit Department.  Walkshed analysis is based on the King County transportation network (TNET) pedestrian features.

name: Centers Designated for Urban and Commercial Land Use in King County
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.The Multicounty and Countywide Planning Policies guide the development of urban centers, which are principally located in cities, and should accommodate concentrations of housing and employment. Focusinggrowth into centers helps achieve the goals of a sustainable and equitable community. name: Development Standards Geographies
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Depicts portions of unincorporated King County area (geographies) where development standards are detailed by specific sections of the King County municipal code (KCC). The geographies are referenced in Title 21A.09. The boundaries of these geographies are defined by the following other GIS layers: Urban Growth Area, Potential Annexation Areas, Cities, and Rural Towns.

name: Health Reporting Areas 2010
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.The 48 Health Reporting Areas (HRAs) are aggregates of Census 2010 blocks created to facilitate the analysis and presentation of sub-county health statistics. Where possible, HRAs are defined as neighborhoods within large cities, smaller cities, unincorporated areas in King County, or a combination of these geographies, with a Census 2010 population over 30,000.

A defined channel or bed is an area that demonstrates clear evidence of the passage of water and includes but is not limited to bedrock channels, gravel beds, sand and silt beds and defined-channel swales. name: King County with Natural Shoreline for Puget Sound and Lake Washington
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.County boundaries for King County, with Puget Sound and Lake Washington removed, showing coastal shoreline and Mercer Island. Also as WASHSH for all of Washington State.

name: Landslide Hazards along River Corridors - Rock avalanche deposit
description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Rock avalanche is a style of landslide characterized by the simultaneous failure of an entire bedrock hillslope and with the dislodged mass cascading to the valley below. Rock avalanches move at extremely high speeds and are potentially massive in size. They can extend farther from the base of slope and cover a much larger area compared with individual rock fall.

description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Points show locations of TDR sending and receiving sites. Sending sites have had unused development potential transferred away, and the sites are protected by conservation easements. Receiving sites have accepted transferred development rights and as a result have more units (or square footage) than base zoning density would allow without the use of TDRs.

description: For more information about this layer please see the GIS Data Catalog.Retaining walls are structures designed to restrain soil to unnatural slopes.
